Forza Milan



2017/2018球季意甲賽程：

常見問題：
AC米蘭同國際米蘭又係有"米蘭"，應該點分？
ANS：AC米蘭簡稱米蘭，國際米蘭叫國米，避免稱呼做AC，因為AC係球會咁解，而milan先係名既簡稱。
AC米蘭仲係咪「老人院」？
ANS：現時全隊平均年齡為25歲。
今個轉會窗到目前為止簽咗咁多球員，遲啲仲會唔會有錢買人？
ANS：其實買球員好少會一炮過畀，多數都係分期，所以仲會有錢簽其他球員。
簽咗咁多球員會唔會過唔到FFP？
ANS：早前米蘭管理層有同UEFA探討經營計劃。
http://sempremilan.com/ts-fassone-meets-uefa-to-discuss-ac-milan-business-plan（By 二石四鳥)[/quote]

Ancelotti: ‘I’m supporting Milan’

Carlo Ancelotti confirms he’s supporting Milan against Juventus in the Coppa Italia final and believes Gennaro Gattuso “is doing very well”.

The 58-year-old has been Coach of both teams, but is synonymous with the Rossoneri having both played for them and enjoyed a successful spell on the bench at San Siro. 

“I’ll watch it with a friend of mine who’s a chef here in Canada,” Ancelotti told Milan TV.  “He’s very much a Milanista so we’ll put the Milan shirt on, that’s what I can do to support the Rossoneri.

“On Wednesday Rino’s team will have a lot of courage, I’d say to the fans to be close to the team, following them with passion.

“That’s what the Milan fans can do very well. It’s clear that this is a delicate moment because the team is a bit behind, but with the right investments and the entrepreneurial qualities the directors have I think they can rise again.

“I met both [CEO Marco] Fassone and [sporting director Massimiliano] Mirabelli last summer, they took on a complicated and difficult situation.

“They need a bit of patience, they’re taking Milan back up but it will take time. The others are running, particularly in Europe.

“There’s a good foundation there and I hope Milan can return as soon as possible.

“Gattuso? He is doing very well, the team has found its identity very quickly. They’ve struggled a lot in the last period, particularly mentally, but Rino will prepare them very well.

“He knows Juventus very well from a tactical point of view, and he’ll motivate his lads exactly as he did when he played.

“The most evident thing is that the team now has an identity in both phases of play.

“I think something that can be improved over time though is a higher press. With a 4-3-3 it’s often difficult to press high.

“In possession the idea of controlling the game is there, I think an improvement would be having more players between the lines and more depth up-front, which is something which has been lacking this year.

Ancelotti also discussed Milan’s opponents, who have won the domestic double for the past three seasons in a row.

“Juventus are a very complicated opponent, very attentive in the defensive phase. I expect a balanced game, I’m not expecting a swashbuckling Juve.

“Milan will have to be good to find spaces in a defence which plays really well collectively, but which has some problems on the individual level.”

Carletto’s daughter, Katia, had a baby son recently and the Curva Sud welcomed little Andrea with a banner.

“They have great affection for me and my family,” Ancelotti said.

“Andrea will be a Milan fan, despite his Neapolitan father. So it’ll be a fight between Parmesan and mozzarella, but I’m sure he’ll prefer Parmesan.”

Falcao swap with Andre Silva?

Milan are working to exchange Andre Silva with Monaco striker Radamel Falcao, as they share the same agent, according to Tuttosport.

The Turin-based newspaper claims on Wednesday’s front page story that representative Jorge Mendes is planning a swap deal over the summer.

Andre Silva is not in the Rossoneri’s plans, just a year after signing him for €38m, and has already been linked with Wolves, but wants a more prestigious club.

That could well be Monaco, who in turn could allow Falcao to leave for a new experience in Serie A.

The Colombia international just turned 32 and has a tally of 24 goals in 35 competitive games for Monaco this season.

His contract runs to June 2020 and Falcao has the kind of rugged determination and work-rate so appreciated by Coach Gennaro Gattuso.

Roma favourites for Kluivert

Roma are the favourites ahead of Milan, Juventus, Manchester United, Arsenal, Tottenham and Chelsea for Justin Kluivert, claim Dutch reports.

The son of former Rossoneri man Patrick Kluivert has been very impressive at Ajax and turned 19 this week.

So far this season, he has contributed 10 goals and five assists in 30 Eredivisie matches.

He is able to play on the left or the right wing.

According to Dutch site VI.nl, Kluivert is heading towards Serie A with Juventus and Milan also interested, but Roma are considered the favourites.

His contract is due to expire in June 2019 and negotiations with agent Mino Raiola have dragged on for months.

Costacurta confirms B teams

FIGC sub-commissioner Alessandro Costacurta confirms Serie A clubs will be allowed to enter B teams into the Lega Pro from next season.

The move has long been wanted by Juventus and the Milan clubs in particular, and it has been increasingly reported in recent weeks that changes would be made.

“Serie A clubs can ask to register their second team in the Lega Pro,” Costacurta confirmed today.

“The B teams will be composed of Under-21 players, with the opportunity to introduce two under-23s outside of the quota.

“Any second team that a Serie A club decides to register will play in the Lega Pro, and will take the place of a club that doesn’t register.

“This year there will be no wild cards [for teams which narrowly miss the registration criteria] and the holes will be filled by the teams who adhere to this announcement.

“And they will have to have a minimum number of players eligible for the Italian national team, a number we’re defining.

“The B teams can be promoted or relegated, with one limitation: they can never play in the same division as the first team.”

好事

"In the first minutes of the first half we still kept our composure, but then we made a mistake, we conceded a goal, then we made more mistakes and it happens, it’s part of the game. However, the team did what they had to do. The full-time score does not tell the whole story because if you think about the first half we created a clear-cut opportunity through Cutrone, whereas they didn’t have many chances: Madzukic’s header and a shot by Dybala’s but I can’t think of other opportunities. We played a good match but mistakes punished us and as I said, it’s part of the game."

"This isn’t new. Juventus give you always the feeling that they can knock you out at any time, because of the type of players they have, because of their mentality. It’s not a coincidence they have been winning all these years. There is a still a long way to go, we still have to work so much to be at their level."

"I’m convinced we are on the right path. We have such a young squad, I’m not making up their ages, they were born in ‘98, ‘97, ‘93, ’94. As I always say, you can’t buy experience on the market, experience is something you build through this type of games, through hard lessons, by performing well. This is fact, it’s been so long since we didn’t have such a young squad with so many young players who are our starters. They are capable of playing quality football and even today we saw it when we had possession of the ball and we were passing it efficiently to each other. However, I repeat, today we have the basis to start building something important and you need time. We will not be signing so many players every summer, instead, we need to trust these boys and continue to improve because they have the quality and a great mentality."

"We will play against two tough sides. On one hand, there is Atalanta who always make it difficult for us because of our local rivalry. Then there is Fiorentina who have obtained great results in the last years so it won’t be easy. Even if we had a tough start to the season and had to wait a lot to finally get to winning ways, we managed to bounce back and just because today we lost, we can’t afford to give up. It is a burning defeat, it burns because we lost, it burns because we conceded four goals, because of the 25 to 30 thousand fans that came to support us and also for those who support AC Milan all around the world. Yet, we need to look forward, face our problems and find the way to solve them."

"After we conceded the fourth goal, I was afraid we would lose 7 or 8 to 0 because when you face such a strong team this is what happens. However, the boys kept trying and created three to four scoring chances… We played the match we wanted but we were unlucky at some points. We made mistakes but as I sais, it’s part of the game. We really wanted to win it, the players, me, my staff, the fans. It was not a great night for anyone supporting AC Milan. Yet, we need to look ahead and strengthen our sense of belonging, be always hungry and become great again".
